# ACP registry → BenchFlow

All **36** agents in the Agent Client Protocol registry (snapshot `v1.0.0`, see [`registry.snapshot.json`](registry.snapshot.json)), classified by whether they can run as a faithful, model-enforced BenchFlow eval. Source of truth: [`src/acp_registry/catalog.py`](src/acp_registry/catalog.py).

**Tally:** wired: 1 · catalog: 20 · native: 5 · vendor-locked: 9 · out-of-scope: 1.

## Wired (1)

`register()` installs these. Routing is verified *by construction* (confirmed env vars + a model format BenchFlow can emit); see the honesty bar in the README for what that does and doesn't claim.

| Agent | id | License | api_protocol | Verified | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| [Qwen Code](https://github.com/QwenLM/qwen-code) | `qwen-code` | Apache-2.0 | `openai-completions` | hello-world (reward 1.0), skillsbench/citation-check (reward 1.0, 33 tool calls) | Cleanest fit in the registry: base URL, key, AND model are all plain env vars (OPENAI_BASE_URL / OPENAI_API_KEY / OPENAI_MODEL), so BenchFlow's env_mapping routes it with no config file and no model-id translation. Verified end-to-end on DeepSeek via Daytona (reward 1.0 on hello-world) with the `acp_model_via_env` fix. **Known issue:** qwen-code advertises an ACP `model` session config option whose values it validates against its OWN model list, so BenchFlow's capability-first dispatch fails when it tries to set the benchmark's model id over ACP (-32603) — seen with both a gateway alias and a bare `deepseek-v4-flash`. The model is already delivered via env (OPENAI_MODEL), so the fix is to NOT drive it over ACP: this package enables BenchFlow's `acp_model_via_env` flag when present. That flag is a proposed BenchFlow change (see the PR); on a build without it, `register()` warns and qwen-code fails at model configuration. |

## Catalog (20)

These can be pointed at an arbitrary OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible base URL, so they're adaptable — each is held back only by something this npx-only first pass doesn't ship (a config-file writer, a binary installer, a uvx bootstrap, or a model-id format BenchFlow can't emit). The **Recipe** column is the exact next step to wire it.

| Agent | id | License | Dist | Recipe to wire |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [goose](https://github.com/block/goose) | `goose` | Apache-2.0 | binary | BYO via OPENAI_HOST + OPENAI_BASE_PATH (or a custom_providers JSON with base_url), GOOSE_PROVIDER=openai, GOOSE_MODEL. Needs a binary installer (Linux x86_64 + aarch64 releases exist) and a base-URL split into host+path, so not in this npx-only first pass. |
| [Stakpak](https://github.com/stakpak/agent) | `stakpak` | Apache-2.0 | binary | BYO via ~/.stakpak/config.toml api_endpoint + model (provider-prefixed, e.g. anthropic/claude-...). Needs a binary installer + a config-file writer (no env-only base URL). Notably implements real session/set_model. |
| [VT Code](https://github.com/vinhnx/VTCode) | `vtcode` | MIT | binary | BYO via vtcode.toml [[custom_providers]] base_url + api_key_env + models. Needs a binary installer + config-file writer. |
| [crow-cli](https://github.com/crow-cli/crow-cli) | `crow-cli` | Apache-2.0 | binary | BYO via ~/.crow/config.yaml provider/base_url/api_key/model (${ENV} interpolation). Needs a binary installer + config-file writer. |
| [Kimi CLI](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/kimi-cli) | `kimi` | MIT | binary | BYO via config.toml [providers.<name>] type=openai_legacy + base_url + api_key (OPENAI_BASE_URL/OPENAI_API_KEY env overrides exist). Ships PyInstaller Linux binaries; needs binary installer + config writer. |
| [Mistral Vibe](https://github.com/mistralai/mistral-vibe) | `mistral-vibe` | Apache-2.0 | binary | BYO via ~/.vibe/config.toml [[providers]] api_base + api_key_env_var. Ships a dedicated vibe-acp Linux binary; needs binary installer + config writer. |
| [Kilo](https://github.com/Kilo-Org/kilocode) | `kilo` | MIT | binary | BYO via config file provider.<id>.options.baseURL + top-level model ({env:VAR} substitution; KILO_API_KEY env). No documented env-only base URL — needs a config-file writer. |
| [Dirac](https://github.com/dirac-run/dirac) | `dirac` | Apache-2.0 | npx | npx, pure-JS — close to wireable. Held back to verify two details first: it reads OPENAI_API_BASE (not OPENAI_BASE_URL), and the model is a launch flag (--model) rather than an env var, so the launch command must interpolate $BENCHFLOW_PROVIDER_MODEL. |
| [DeepAgents](https://github.com/langchain-ai/deepagentsjs) | `deepagents` | MIT | npx | npx, pure-JS, and advertises an ACP 'model' config option — but model ids are provider-prefixed with a COLON (e.g. openai:gpt-...), a format BenchFlow can't currently emit (it does bare / provider-slash / registered-provider-slash). Base URL rides the underlying LangChain client's OPENAI_BASE_URL. Wire once a colon format is supported. |
| [fast-agent](https://github.com/evalstate/fast-agent) | `fast-agent` | Apache-2.0 | uvx | BYO via OPENAI_BASE_URL/ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (config or OPENAI__ nested env) + --model. uvx-distributed, so it needs a uv bootstrap this npx-only first pass doesn't ship. |
| [Autohand Code](https://github.com/autohandai/autohand-acp) | `autohand` | Apache-2.0 | npx | BYO via ~/.autohand/config.json provider/baseUrl/apiKey/model (AUTOHAND_CONFIG, AUTOHAND_MODEL). Needs a config-file writer. |
| [Nova](https://github.com/Compass-Agentic-Platform/nova) | `nova` | proprietary | npx | npx — close to wireable via ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL or DEFAULT_OPENAI_BASE_URL + model env. Held back to first verify the optional Compass routing layer can be fully bypassed with raw ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL (it bundles @anthropic-ai/sdk). |
| [Cline](https://github.com/cline/cline) | `cline` | Apache-2.0 | npx | BYO via `cline auth --provider openai-native --baseurl ... --modelid ...`, but multiple open issues report the custom base URL being ignored (calls still hit api.openai.com). Wire only after verifying requests actually reach the gateway. |
| [GitHub Copilot](https://github.com/github/copilot-cli) | `github-copilot-cli` | proprietary | npx | BYO via COPILOT_PROVIDER_BASE_URL/_TYPE/_API_KEY + COPILOT_MODEL (all env — would be wireable). Held back for two reasons: the npm package is an npm-loader that fetches a platform binary at runtime (the _js_agent_install `node <bin>` wrapper may not apply), and BYOK-in-ACP was broken <=1.0.60 (fixed ~1.0.61) — must pin + smoke-test session/new. |
| Codebuddy Code (`https://www.codebuddy.ai`) | `codebuddy-code` | proprietary | npx | BYO via models.json url + apiKey (${ENV} refs), CODEBUDDY_API_KEY. Default first run wants a Tencent login. Needs a config-file writer; chat-completions only. |
| DimCode (`https://dim.qwenkimi.com`) | `dimcode` | proprietary | npx | BYO via `dim provider add --base-url --api-key --model` (~/.dimcode/v2/providers.json) — no LLM-routing env vars, so it needs a config-file writer. Per-provider wire protocol undocumented (binary is minified/closed). |
| Grok Build (`https://x.ai/cli`) | `grok-build` | proprietary | binary | BYO via GROK_MODELS_BASE_URL + per-model config (base_url/env_key). Held back: proprietary, access-gated (SuperGrok/Premium+), and shell-installer-only (no public per-arch release page), though the installer fetches both Linux arches. |
| [Junie](https://github.com/JetBrains/junie) | `junie` | proprietary | binary | BYO via $JUNIE_HOME/models/*.json (id/baseUrl/apiType/apiKey) + --model custom:<id>. Needs a binary (shell-installer) + JSON writer. Proprietary (JetBrains AI Service ToS). |
| [Poolside](https://github.com/poolsideai/pool) | `poolside` | proprietary | binary | BYO via POOLSIDE_API_URL / --api-url + --model. Needs a binary (shell-installer) path; CLI binary is proprietary. |
| [Minion Code](https://github.com/femto/minion-code) | `minion-code` | AGPL-3.0 | uvx | uvx-distributed (needs a uv bootstrap). BYO provider config UNCONFIRMED from public docs — confirm before wiring. AGPL-3.0. |

## Native (5)

BenchFlow ships these directly; use `--agent <built-in>`. Listed so the registry mapping is complete.

| Agent | id | BenchFlow built-in |
|---|---|---|
| [Claude Agent](https://github.com/agentclientprotocol/claude-agent-acp) | `claude-acp` | `--agent claude-agent-acp` |
| [Codex CLI](https://github.com/zed-industries/codex-acp) | `codex-acp` | `--agent codex-acp` |
| [Gemini CLI](https://github.com/google-gemini/gemini-cli) | `gemini` | `--agent gemini` |
| [OpenCode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) | `opencode` | `--agent opencode` |
| [pi ACP](https://github.com/svkozak/pi-acp) | `pi-acp` | `--agent pi-acp` |

## Vendor-Locked (9)

Authenticate only to their vendor's backend (no arbitrary base URL), so BenchFlow can't enforce the benchmark's model or capture usage. Not a faithful eval.

| Agent | id | License | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Amp](https://github.com/tao12345666333/amp-acp) | `amp-acp` | Apache-2.0 | Thin wrapper over Amp, a managed Sourcegraph service: AMP_API_KEY only, no base-URL override, fixed curated model set (modes smart/deep/rush). Cannot enforce the benchmark's model. |
| [Auggie CLI](https://github.com/augmentcode/auggie) | `auggie` | proprietary | Requires an Augment account + `auggie login`. AUGMENT_API_URL targets Augment tenants only; the LLM call is server-side. No BYO key or base URL. |
| Cortex Code (`https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/cortex-code/cortex-code`) | `cortex-code` | proprietary | Requires a Snowflake account + CORTEX_USER role; models run inside Snowflake Cortex. No BYO base URL. |
| [Corust Agent](https://github.com/Corust-ai/corust-agent-release) | `corust-agent` | GPL-3.0-or-later | Runs Corust's own fine-tuned model through a Corust-hosted gateway; no custom base URL or arbitrary model. Also ships no arm64 Linux binary (x86_64 only) and is GPL-3.0. |
| Cursor (`https://cursor.com/docs/cli`) | `cursor` | proprietary | Headless cursor-agent routes only through Cursor's backend (requires a Cursor account). The app's custom-OpenAI-base-URL BYOK is chat-models-only and does not reach the CLI. |
| Factory Droid (`https://factory.ai`) | `factory-droid` | proprietary | Requires a Factory account; model selection and the LLM call are Factory-managed. No documented BYO base URL. |
| [GLM Agent](https://github.com/stefandevo/glm-acp-agent) | `glm-acp-agent` | Apache-2.0 | A base-URL var (ACP_GLM_BASE_URL) exists, but the README states it is built for the Z.AI GLM Coding Plan and the endpoint rejects any model Z.AI hasn't whitelisted — effectively locked to GLM models. |
| Qoder CLI (`https://qoder.com`) | `qoder` | proprietary | Auth only via `qodercli login` or a Qoder PAT; no arbitrary base URL. Models are Qoder-managed. |
| [siGit Code](https://github.com/getsigit/sigit) | `sigit` | Apache-2.0 | 'Runs on your machine. No API keys. No cloud round-trips' — downloads a local GGUF model (Onde Inference). No remote-provider concept at all, so nothing to route through the gateway. |

## Out-Of-Scope (1)

Not a single LLM coding/eval agent.

| Agent | id | License | Why |
|---|---|---|---|
| [Agoragentic](https://github.com/rhein1/agoragentic-integrations) | `agoragentic-acp` | MIT | Not a single LLM agent with a routable model — it's a marketplace/payments surface. Out of scope for model-enforced evals. |

---

Routing facts were researched from each agent's own docs/source on 2026-06-13 (the `source` field on each catalog entry). Re-run `scripts/refresh_registry.py` to diff against the live registry.
